page: UpCloud HIMEM-4xCPU-32GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 16 MB of L3 cache. The server is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 100 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear trade-off between memory capacity and processing speed. The shared AMD EPYC processor delivers poor single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 784.49 and weak multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3115.76. Memory bandwidth is average for read-only operations, reaching 415.18 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 41.36 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth is weak at 19.09 GB/sec. Computational tasks like software compilation and ray tracing score in the weak tier, while text processing and compression tasks achieve average marks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU model allows UpCloud to offer a high-density memory profile at a lower entry point than dedicated-core instances. However, the shared CPU allocation and weak database operations benchmark make it less suitable for high-throughput transactional databases or intensive CPU workloads. This server is best suited for memory-intensive but CPU-light workloads, such as caching layers, lightweight web serving, small-scale LLM inference with small models, and general-purpose applications requiring a larger memory footprint without sustained processing demands.
description: UpCloud HIMEM-4xCPU-32GB is a memory-optimized virtual server configured with 4 vCPUs on a shared AMD EPYC 7542 processor and 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance includes 100 GB of local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark data reveals a trade-off: while it offers a generous 8.0 GB of memory per core, its shared CPU allocation results in weak processing performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score in the bottom 10% and weak multi-core results. Memory read bandwidth remains average, but write speeds and database operations are relatively weak. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy, CPU-light applications. It is best suited for caching layers, memory-intensive background utilities, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: UpCloud HIMEM-4xCPU-32GB is a memory-optimized KVM virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and a 100 GB local SSD, designed for memory-intensive, CPU-light workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio and bundled SSD storage for memory-intensive, CPU-light workloads.

High memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU
Best suited for caching, lightweight web serving, and memory-heavy utilities
